drafts.subject: Centrica RFP IT-INF-DC-COOL-2026 — feedback on your bid
drafts.body:
Dear Klaus-Peter,

Thank you for the time and effort your team invested in NorthernAir's response to Centrica's Data Centre Cooling RFP (reference IT-INF-DC-COOL-2026). The Procurement Council met on 28 July and I am writing to confirm that, on this occasion, we are not progressing NorthernAir to contract award.

The Council recognised the genuine engineering strengths of your Adiabatic-Plus solution — most notably the 1.1 L/kWh make-up water target, which is the best of the field and a meaningful capability for our Welsh sites. The decision was driven by three points where your bid sat below where we needed it for this specific procurement:

1. UK 24/7 critical-response engineering. Your bid showed 38 UK engineers, with Cluster C night-shift sub-contracted to a partner who was unnamed at submission. Centrica's TPRM gating could not be cleared on that basis.

2. SLA evidence. The 24-month track record of 96.8% / 92.4% on response and fix sat below the floor we set in the RFP for this category (98% / 95%).

3. Refrigerant phase-down. The proposed 2032 transition to GWP < 150 refrigerants is two years beyond Centrica's contractual covenant of 2030.

We would welcome NorthernAir's continued engagement on water-led procurements where the adiabatic capability is centre-stage. Our Battery Energy Storage and Heat Pump categories also have water-stewardship requirements where your team's pedigree would be strongly relevant; Helena Wright on my team will be in touch separately.

Thank you again for the bid and for the professionalism of your team throughout the process.

Best regards,
Priya Rai
Senior Category Manager, IT & Digital
Centrica plc
